title: Load any R packages you need for your thesis

markdown.headingText: Load any R packages you need for your thesis
markdown.markdown:
<!--
This is the introduction chapter for your Reed College thesis.

All thesis metadata (title, author, date, institution, division, department,
advisor, degree, abstract, acknowledgements, dedication, preface) is now
configured in _quarto.yml. Edit that file to customize your thesis.

If you'd prefer to not include a Dedication, for example, simply delete or
comment out that section in _quarto.yml.
-->

```{r}
#| label: include_packages
#| include: false
# This Quarto template is based on the Reed College thesis format
library(knitr)
library(ggplot2)
```

<!--
Chapter files are listed in _quarto.yml in the order they should appear.
Front matter (acknowledgements, preface, abstract, dedication) is defined
in _quarto.yml and will be automatically included in the PDF output.
-->

<!-- The {.unnumbered} option here means that the introduction will be "Chapter 0." You can also use {-} for no numbers
on chapters.
-->

# Introduction {.unnumbered}

Welcome to the _Quarto_ thesis template. This template is based on the Reed College LaTeX template, but provides a nicer interface for those that have never used TeX or LaTeX before. Using _Quarto_ will also allow you to easily keep track of your analyses in **R** or **Python** code chunks, with the resulting plots and output included as well. The hope is this _Quarto_ template gets you in the habit of doing reproducible research, which benefits you long-term as a researcher, but also will greatly help anyone that is trying to reproduce or build onto your results down the road.

Hopefully, you won't have much of a learning period to go through and you will reap the benefits of a nicely formatted thesis. The use of LaTeX in combination with _Markdown_ is more consistent than the output of a word processor, much less prone to corruption or crashing, and the resulting file is smaller than a Word file. While you may have never had problems using Word in the past, your thesis is likely going to be about twice as large and complex as anything you've written before, taxing Word's capabilities. After working with _Quarto_ for a few weeks, we are confident this will be your reporting style of choice going forward.

<!--
If you're still on the fence about using _Quarto_, check out the Quarto documentation at <https://quarto.org/>
-->

**Why use it?**

_Quarto_ creates a simple and straightforward way to interface with the beauty of LaTeX. In addition to creating a user friendly interface to LaTeX, _Quarto_ also allows you to read in your data, analyze it, and visualize it using **R** or **Python**, and also to provide the documentation and commentary on the results of your project. Further, it allows for computed results to be passed inline to the commentary of your results. You'll see more on this later.

<!-- 
Having your code and commentary all together in one place has a plethora of benefits!
-->

**Who should use it?**

Anyone who needs to use data analysis, math, tables, a lot of figures, complex cross-references, or who just cares about the final appearance of their document should use _Quarto_. Of particular use should be anyone in the sciences, but the user-friendly nature of _Markdown_ and its ability to keep track of and easily include figures, automatically generate a table of contents, index, references, table of figures, etc. should make it of great benefit to nearly anyone writing a thesis project.

**For additional help with Quarto**
Please visit [the Quarto documentation](https://quarto.org/docs/guide/).

language.appendix-attribution-cite-as: For attribution, please cite this work as:

metadata.thesis-abstract:
This is your abstract text. You can write it here directly.
It can span multiple paragraphs.
metadata.thesis-acknowledgements: I want to thank a few people.
metadata.thesis-dedication: You can have a dedication here if you wish.
metadata.thesis-preface:
This is an example of a thesis setup to use the Reed thesis document class
(for LaTeX) and Quarto.
